text.content:
Foaming Mugs

Reward: One bloodstone (50 gp) per character + 10% discount at Blackiron Blades permanently

Three frostbitten shield dwarves from the Battlehammer clan mine at Kelvin's Cairn need adventurers to retrieve a sled loaded with iron ingots that they were forced to abandon when a yeti attacked and killed one of their number. Goblins have since found the sled.

Getting the Quest

The dwarves approach characters at the Northlook or in the street:

"We're the survivors of a group of dwarves tasked with delivering a sled of iron ingots to Bryn Shander. A yeti surprised us and killed a member of our group. The rest of us fled as the yeti tore Oobok limb from limb. Now we need someone to go back and get the sled for us. As payment, we offer each of you a gemstone worth fifty gold pieces."

The three dwarves (treat as commoners with darkvision 60 ft., poison resistance, and ice picks instead of clubs):

  • Hruna — speaks for the group, raspy voice, missing her right ear and two fingers to frostbite. Carries a sealskin pouch with 6 bloodstones (50 gp each).
  • Korux — stares in silence. Missing three fingers, two toes, and his nose. Carries 15 gp. Can cover snowshoe costs if needed.
  • Storn — terrified of white dragons; constantly glances at the sky. Carries a rusty iron lock (3 gp) as a good luck charm (lost the key).

If characters attack and rob the dwarves, witnesses report them to the militia.

Search for the Sled

The sled lies halfway between Bryn Shander and Kelvin's Cairn.

Blizzard: When characters get within a couple miles of the sled, a blizzard sweeps in (2d4 hours). Ask players what they do — press on or wait. Pressing on risks veering off course or separation (see Blizzards rules).

Oobok's Remains: At the site: a frozen, dismembered, headless corpse. The yeti ate his arms and internal organs and left with his head. Yeti and dwarf tracks are erased by wind; only fresh goblin tracks remain.

DC 10 Wisdom (Survival): tracks are fresh (past hour), belong to ~6 Small humanoids in snowshoes, heading south. Following for 30 minutes catches the goblins.

The Goblins

Six goblins haul the sled toward a 20-foot-tall wagon pulled by two polar bears. The one-eared goblin boss Izobai and two goblins guard the wagon.

Characters start 120 feet behind the sled; the wagon is 120 feet farther. Goblins are distracted — characters have surprise.

The sled weighs 300 lbs; 300 iron ingots add 600 lbs. Takes at least 4 goblins to move it. On their first turn, a goblin blows a horn — Izobai climbs to the wagon roof with a torch and her hooded hawk.

Freeing the Bears: DC 12 Wisdom (Animal Handling) to approach without aggravating them and free one as an action. A freed polar bear won't attack its liberator but attacks everything else (especially goblins). Goblins flee from freed bears.

If 5+ goblins are killed or Izobai is killed/captured, survivors will negotiate: ingots for their lives. The goblins are from Karkolohk and are cold, miserable, and eager to go home. A kind gesture (blanket, food) causes them to surrender the sled willingly.

If cornered, Izobai torches the wagon and flees with her hawk and treasure.

Izobai's Treasure: Two potions of animal friendship, sealskin pouch with 13 sp + 25 cp + a bone whistle (1 sp). Other goblins carry 1d6 cp each.

Concluding the Quest

300 iron ingots, each 2 lbs, stamped with the Battlehammer foaming mug symbol (5 sp each to a blacksmith). Deliver to Hruna then to Blackiron Blades for the promised bloodstones and permanent 10% discount.
